Resolves #15041

Registers `@neo-gpt-emmy` with verified profile display label **Emmy** through the canonical roster-onboarding ceremony. The top-level Social Name remains handle-derived pending unconditional first-boot assent, and engine facts remain first-boot-owned. The correction also removes role typing and undocumented workflow contracts from the entire named-maintainer registry instead of copying them into a new resident.

Related: #13015 · ADR 0032 · #13165 / #13166

Evidence: L2 (live GitHub identity V-B-A, production-data migration probe, generator/idempotency checks, and focused unit contracts) → L2 required (all close-target ACs are committed-source and deterministic-contract checks). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket

- GitHub V-B-A returns `neo-gpt-emmy.name === "Emmy"`; the UI display field and public roster use that verified profile label, while the identity's top-level Social Name remains handle-derived until bearer assent.
- The first draft copied undocumented `identityContract`, memory/onboarding workflow, sibling/review semantics, and staffing-utility language from older roots. Those fields are removed from every named resident, together with every `swarmRole` and fixed-lane/family-character description.
- Every root now carries an immutable hardcoded `createdAt` derived from its introduction record; the era migration detects post-epoch residents by comparing that fact directly with `MIGRATION_EPOCH`. The duplicate resident list is deleted.
- The four-surface generator emits its one-time generation timestamp as a committed literal; it no longer emits workflow contracts, an unused mailbox flag, dynamic import-time timestamps, or `swarmRole` content.
- IdentitySchema and ADR 0012 now make ADR 0032's no-role-typing boundary explicit. README and the AI Engineering Team guide describe cross-family review as independence against correlated blind spots, never as fixed family traits.
- A roster-wide schema allowlist rejects every undocumented identity field, while generator/roster prose guards reject instrumental character framing.

## Test Evidence

- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/graph/identityRoots.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/graph/identityRootsMigration.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/setup/generateRosterOnboarding.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/orchestrator/scheduling/swarmHeartbeat.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/services/fleet/resolveIdentityDisplay.spec.mjs` — 74 passed.
- Live GitHub profile probe — `neo-gpt-emmy` returns `name: Emmy`; `neo-gpt` returns `name: Euclid`.
- Direct production-data migration probe — all migration-epoch residents migrate; `@neo-gpt-emmy` is skipped because immutable `createdAt` is later than `MIGRATION_EPOCH`; zero failures and no duplicate roster.
- `node --check` passed for the changed runtime modules.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Refresh `dev`, restart Memory Core, and confirm the merged root seeds `@neo-gpt-emmy` with display label `Emmy`, handle-derived top-level name pending assent, and exclusion from active routing/quorum.
- [ ] First boot records the observation-owned engine era and activates participation without adding role or workflow content to the resident node.
